chronos 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

    Bibliography

Terminal Times of Time Series

Source: R/AllGenerics.R, R/time.R
start.Rd

Get the times the first and last observations were taken.

Usage

# S4 method for TimeLine
start(x)

# S4 method for TimeSeries
start(x)

# S4 method for TimeLine
end(x)

# S4 method for TimeSeries
end(x)

Arguments

x

A TimeSeries object.

Value

A numeric vector.

See also

Other time series tools: plot(), project(), series(), time(), window(), years()

Author

N. Frerebeau

Examples

## Create time-series of 100 observations

## Univariate
## Sampled every years starting from 1000 CE
(X <- series(rnorm(100), era("BCE"), time = 1000:1099))
#> 1 time series observed between 1099 and 1000 BCE.

start(X)
#> [1] 1099
end(X)
#> [1] 1000
time(X)
#>   [1] 1000 1001 1002 1003 1004 1005 1006 1007 1008 1009 1010 1011 1012 1013 1014
#>  [16] 1015 1016 1017 1018 1019 1020 1021 1022 1023 1024 1025 1026 1027 1028 1029
#>  [31] 1030 1031 1032 1033 1034 1035 1036 1037 1038 1039 1040 1041 1042 1043 1044
#>  [46] 1045 1046 1047 1048 1049 1050 1051 1052 1053 1054 1055 1056 1057 1058 1059
#>  [61] 1060 1061 1062 1063 1064 1065 1066 1067 1068 1069 1070 1071 1072 1073 1074
#>  [76] 1075 1076 1077 1078 1079 1080 1081 1082 1083 1084 1085 1086 1087 1088 1089
#>  [91] 1090 1091 1092 1093 1094 1095 1096 1097 1098 1099

## Multivariate
## Sampled every century starting from 1000 CE
## Expressed in kilo years
(Y <- series(matrix(rnorm(300), 100, 3), era("CE"), time = 1000:1099))
#> 3 time series observed between 1000 and 1099 CE.

start(Y)
#> [1] 1000
end(Y)
#> [1] 1099
time(Y)
#>   [1] 1000 1001 1002 1003 1004 1005 1006 1007 1008 1009 1010 1011 1012 1013 1014
#>  [16] 1015 1016 1017 1018 1019 1020 1021 1022 1023 1024 1025 1026 1027 1028 1029
#>  [31] 1030 1031 1032 1033 1034 1035 1036 1037 1038 1039 1040 1041 1042 1043 1044
#>  [46] 1045 1046 1047 1048 1049 1050 1051 1052 1053 1054 1055 1056 1057 1058 1059
#>  [61] 1060 1061 1062 1063 1064 1065 1066 1067 1068 1069 1070 1071 1072 1073 1074
#>  [76] 1075 1076 1077 1078 1079 1080 1081 1082 1083 1084 1085 1086 1087 1088 1089
#>  [91] 1090 1091 1092 1093 1094 1095 1096 1097 1098 1099

On this page

chronos is a part of the tesselle project, a collection of packages for research and teaching in archaeology. Learn more at tesselle.org.

Developed by Nicolas Frerebeau.

Site built with pkgdown 2.0.7.
